title: Chat Admin Logger
description: A Minecraft Plugin That Logges Everything What Admins Does in Chat
body:
AdminLogger is a powerful yet lightweight Minecraft server plugin designed to bring transparency and accountability to your server administration. It automatically logs all important operator (OP) commands and admin actions directly into the in-game chat, so both players and staff can see what changes are being made in real time.

This plugin is perfect for servers that want to keep their community informed about administrative actions such as game mode changes, item giving, teleportations, bans, kicks, and more — all without cluttering console logs or requiring external tools.

Why choose AdminLogger?
Instant transparency: Players see admin actions as they happen, promoting trust and fairness.

Wide command coverage: Supports most commonly used OP commands including /gamemode, /give, /tp, /ban, /kick, /op, /deop, /effect, and many others.

Creative mode item logging: Tracks when admins or players in creative mode pick up items, adding an extra layer of oversight.

Color-coded, clear messages: Makes it easy to spot admin actions in chat.

Multilingual support: Configurable messages with English and Turkish by default.

Lightweight & easy to use: No extra dependencies, simple setup, and optimized for minimal server impact.

Open-source: Freely available under the MIT License for anyone to customize or contribute.

How does it work?
Whenever an admin uses a monitored command, AdminLogger sends a clear, formatted message to the server chat showing who performed the action and what they did. For example:

Changing game mode:
[AdminLogger] Finzzy changed game mode SURVIVAL to CREATIVE.

Giving items:
[AdminLogger] Finzzy gave Player123 64x DIAMOND.

Teleporting players:
[AdminLogger] Finzzy teleported Player123.

Picking up items in creative mode:
[AdminLogger] Finzzy took 16x STONE in creative mode.

AdminLogger enhances server moderation by making operator actions visible to the entire community, fostering a safer and more accountable environment. It's ideal for public, semi-private, or private servers looking to maintain transparency and player trust.

Compatible with Paper 1.20 and 1.21+. Regularly maintained and open for contributions.
